extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked final climb Col du Granon, dropped POGAČAR who lost 2:51, taking yellow by over 2 minutes.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL and VAN AERT sprint collaboration at km 16.5 shows green jersey defense requires early breakaway commitment on mountain stages.
BENOOT placing jumbo-Visma rider in early break provides summit ally for VINGEGAARD, revealing pre-race orchestrated mountain stage setup.
POGAČAR yo-yoed repeatedly on Galibier before final collapse, showing accumulation across multiple climbs matters more than single attack.
QUINTANA jumped to third overall with sub-minute gap, demonstrating aging climbers exploit chaos when favorites implode on unprecedented climbs.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: First Tour stage win coinciding with yellow jersey capture shows confidence threshold crossed, power dynamics shifted permanently.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: Lost 2:51 after dominating first 10 stages, cracked on first high-altitude summit finish reveals altitude or accumulation weakness.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Committed to 16km breakaway for green jersey points on hardest mountain stage proves versatility extends beyond expected limits.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Recovered from Galibier struggles to finish fourth at 2:04, yo-yo resilience suggests GC podium threat remains despite mid-stage difficulties.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Moved to third overall within one minute of second, renaissance confirmed on highest Tour summit since career peak years.
rider_signals.GAUDU David: neutral: Strong performance maintained top-10 GC despite dropping one place, consistent climbing without breakthrough.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: Dropped to sixth overall despite solid ride, illustrates depth compression when favorites crack creates reshuffling.
